## Code Review - Issue abfd6016

I have completed a comprehensive review of the implementation against the success criteria, architecture requirements, and code quality standards. Here are my findings:

### Gate Status (Tier 1)
- ✅ **cargo-ci**: PASSED (last updated 2026-04-24T09:12:39)
- ✅ **npm-ci**: PASSED (last updated 2026-04-24T12:27:01)  
- ❌ **code-review**: FAILED (last updated 2026-04-24T12:27:27)

**AUTOMATIC FAIL** - One required gate has not passed.

### Success Criteria Review (Tier 2)

**✅ Server returns Content-Type: text/html for .html documents**

- Test coverage: `test_infer_content_type_html()` at line 1161

**✅ jit document add persists format: "html"**

## VERDICT: FAIL

**Reason:** Code-review gate shows status "failed" in the issue JSON. According to Tier 1 requirements, this is an automatic fail regardless of code quality.

**Note:** The actual implementation appears to satisfy all success criteria based on code inspection and passing tests. However, the gate system shows code-review as failed, which blocks acceptance per the lead review protocol.The secondary diff review finished, so I’m folding that into the final verdict now.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **Server-side HTML support is complete.** `crates/server/src/routes.rs:31-46` registers both raw endpoints, `routes.rs:309-327` infers `.html`/`.htm` as `text/html`, `routes.rs:347-368` returns that type in JSON content responses, and `routes.rs:377-434` serves raw bytes with `Content-Type` plus CSP. Coverage is in `routes.rs:1160-1164`, `1242-1284`, and `1360-1397`.
- **Web renderer registry and delegation are in place.** `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:17-47` defines the ordered registry and `pickRenderer()`. `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.tsx:93-154` delegates rendering through the registry and no longer imports `react-markdown`; markdown behavior lives in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/MarkdownRenderer.tsx:1-160`. Dispatch and markdown-smoke coverage are in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/renderers.test.tsx:18-37` and `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.test.tsx:75-106`.
- **`HtmlRenderer` matches the story contract.** `web/src/components/Document/renderers/HtmlRenderer.tsx:14-49` renders the label/path toolbar, “Open in new tab” link with `_blank` + `noopener`, and a sandboxed iframe with `allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-popups`. Raw URL generation is centralized in `web/src/api/client.ts:133-167`, with tests at `web/src/api/client.test.ts:177-219` and `web/src/components/Document/renderers/HtmlRenderer.test.tsx:43-175`.
- **Rust adapter registration and persistence are correct.** `crates/jit/src/document/adapter.rs:53-147` adds `HtmlAdapter`, `adapter.rs:177-183` registers it in `with_builtins()`, and `adapter.rs:638-642` verifies `.resolve("foo.html", "") -> "html"`. `crates/jit/src/commands/document.rs:33-76` persists the detected format, with integration coverage at `crates/jit/tests/validate_document_tests.rs:98-127`.
- **Child-task gate criterion is satisfied.** The four child issues are all `done` with `cargo-ci` and `code-review` passed: `.jit/issues/75b1ccb8-6796-41da-9a96-fa95ed96cc94.json:5-23`, `.jit/issues/42bd50ce-7cd9-47e6-993f-2b9bb7c16a35.json:5-23`, `.jit/issues/eeafaa78-290d-4390-9097-4d29ff48ef82.json:5-26`, `.jit/issues/85bd1288-6d6b-4f56-8d2c-ca859df7e92c.json:5-23`.

## Architecture and Separation of Concerns

- The server changes stay in the route layer and delegate I/O to `CommandExecutor` rather than touching files directly: `crates/server/src/routes.rs:287-305`, `355-361`, `384-390`, `415-421`.
- The web refactor improves separation instead of adding one-off HTML special cases: `DocumentViewer` is now shell/orchestration (`web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.tsx:17-169`), while renderers are isolated in `renderers/`.
- I found **no boundary violations** and **no new technical debt** in the touched design.

## Functional Paradigm

- The renderer selection is data-driven and extensible: `rendererRegistry.find(...)` in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:29-46` is a clean first-match-wins implementation.
- The Rust changes remain expression-oriented; the imperative code is confined to HTTP response assembly at the I/O boundary (`crates/server/src/routes.rs:392-403`, `423-433`).

## Correctness and Invariants

- Raw endpoints are byte-faithful: `crates/server/src/routes.rs:392-403` and `423-433` return `Body::from(bytes)`, with non-UTF8 coverage at `routes.rs:1294-1355`.
- CSP is explicit and allows `https:` as required: `crates/server/src/routes.rs:330`.
- HTML documents correctly suppress history via renderer metadata: `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:29-35` and `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.tsx:95-145`.
- No PID-handling code was touched, and I found no new violations around atomic writes, gate semantics, assignee format, or label format.

## Safety

- I found **no Rust `unsafe` code** in the reviewed implementation. The touched production paths also avoid `unwrap()`/`expect()` panics; header parsing uses a safe fallback in `crates/server/src/routes.rs:394-398` and `424-428`.

## Testing

- The Rust validation run completed cleanly: the captured output shows repository validation passed and Rust tests passed (`/tmp/copilot-tool-output-1777039839335-fcylqc.txt:1`, `:84-85`, `:781`).
- Targeted coverage exists for all new behavior:
  - server raw/content handling: `crates/server/src/routes.rs:1160-1164`, `1242-1284`, `1294-1355`, `1360-1397`, `1666-1720`
  - renderer dispatch: `web/src/components/Document/renderers/renderers.test.tsx:18-37`
  - HTML renderer behavior: `web/src/components/Document/renderers/HtmlRenderer.test.tsx:43-175`
  - `DocumentViewer` markdown/HTML smoke coverage: `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.test.tsx:75-221`
  - CLI persistence of `format: "html"`: `crates/jit/tests/validate_document_tests.rs:98-127`
- I could not complete a local rerun of the web scripts because this environment denies execution for those commands, so per your instructions I fell back to the issue-scoped gate history. The scoped `npm-ci` gate is passed in `.jit/issues/abfd6016-a79d-4a5c-9d1d-d5393e7acb2d.json:19-33`, so I do **not** treat that environment limitation as a code failure.

## Error Handling

- The new server paths preserve contextual error logging and map failures through `path_read_error_status()` at `crates/server/src/routes.rs:357-361`, `387-390`, `418-421`.
- `add_document_reference()` warns on unreadable documents instead of silently pretending asset scanning succeeded: `crates/jit/src/commands/document.rs:57-63`.
- No error-handling regression found.

## CLI Contract

- The relevant CLI path still supports `--json`: `crates/jit/src/main.rs:1985-2012` threads `json` through `doc add` and emits structured JSON output.

## Documentation

- The new public Rust API is documented with examples: `crates/jit/src/document/adapter.rs:53-77` (`HtmlAdapter`) and `149-164` (`AdapterRegistry`).
- No documentation debt was introduced in the touched public API surface.

## Prior Review Feedback

- The most recent failed review was centered on web validation not completing in the review environment. That condition reproduces here as an execution-permission problem, not as a code defect.
- Given your explicit instruction to rely on scoped run history when the environment cannot run those checks, and with `npm-ci` already passed at `.jit/issues/abfd6016-a79d-4a5c-9d1d-d5393e7acb2d.json:19-33`, I consider that prior feedback **addressed/non-blocking** rather than an unresolved defect.

## JIT Issue Management

- `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed (`/tmp/copilot-tool-output-1777039839335-fcylqc.txt:1`).
- The parent issue’s prerequisite dependencies are recorded at `.jit/issues/abfd6016-a79d-4a5c-9d1d-d5393e7acb2d.json:8-12`, and the child-task gate state is fully green in the child issue files cited above.
